children.text: This week, we turn toward more-than-human worlds. Plants, rivers, ice, wind, rain, and weather enter the room as collaborators. We expand last week’s scale of perspective from individual voice to planetary systems, stretching “Asian American” into transpacific histories entangled with empire, climate systems, migration, and sovereignty.

How do we listen when the planet speaks? On Monday, we begin with environmental archive, move through climate testimony, and end with interspecies collaboration. To prep, explore the multimedia in this module.
